quoted_text:
BREAKING: US officials have now confirmed in a briefing that Iran will get full access to a total of $100 billion in frozen funds and the $300 billion reconstruction fund, both included in the deal with implementation now underway, per WSJ.

More than $150 billion of the fund has already been committed, directly contradicting Trump's claims that the US would not contribute to the $300 billion fund, per Reuters.

Because API reported -8.33 million bbls, EIA will report between -9 to -10 million bbls.

SPR release of 8.9 million bbls.

Total crude draw of 17.9 to 18.9 million bbls, or the largest crude draw in history.

quoted_text:
It is true that Iran's power to close the strait is potent.

And yet...the oil market was consistently bullish re: the likelihood of a deal that would get the strait back open. Brent had a hard time living above $100-110. That's high, but not disastrously high. Possible to overstate this source of leverage.

Imo, demonstrating an ability and will to lay waste to the Gulf may ultimately prove a more potent deterrent.
